concerns.text: Task T034 implements a permutation test with n=1000 iterations, but spec FR-007 explicitly mandates n=100. There is no task covering the exact n=100 requirement, creating a gap between the spec and the implementation plan.

Task T027 implements a multi-class classifier for 'Retraction-Only' labels. The spec (FR-004, FR-005, FR-006) only defines binary prediction (Fragile vs Robust) and a citation baseline. No task exists to implement the binary classification logic as strictly defined, while T027 addresses an unrequested scope.

Task T037 makes covariate adjustment conditional ('ONLY IF... inconclusive'), but spec FR-012 mandates it as a required step ('System MUST perform...'). No task covers the mandatory execution of covariate adjustment for confounding control.

Task T035 describes a threshold sweep over 'a range of values', but spec FR-008 and SC-002 explicitly require the specific set {0.3, 0.5, 0.7}. No task explicitly covers the mandatory execution of this specific threshold set.

The spec's edge case 'What happens when the external retraction database has no entries' requires an abort with a specific error message. While T019 mentions aborting if 'no ground truth labels found', it does not explicitly cover the specific error message or the strict abort condition for the scientific analysis path vs synthetic fallback.
